title: vicia venosa subsp. stolonifera
intro: Vicia venosa subsp. stolonifera, also known as creeping vetch, is a subspecies of perennial flowering plant in the pea family that grows in various regions around the world. Its medicinal properties are attributed to its high content of alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of creeping vetch include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable herb for treating conditions such as wounds, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of creeping vetch include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
